description:
You are a mission designer sizing the propulsion system for the AeroCom-1 satellite. It will be launched into GTO and use an Electric Propulsion (EP) system to continuously spiral out to GEO. Simulate this multi-month finite burn in GMAT to determine the exact time-of-flight and propellant consumed.

Spacecraft 'AeroCom1' (DryMass: 1500 kg) initial state (EarthMJ2000Eq):
- Epoch: 01 Jan 2026 12:00:00.000 UTC
- SMA: 24392.5 km, ECC: 0.730, INC: 27.0 deg, RAAN: 0.0 deg, AOP: 180.0 deg, TA: 0.0 deg

Propulsion Configuration:
- ChemicalTank 'XenonTank': FuelMass = 500 kg
- ChemicalThruster 'EPThruster': Thrust (C1) = 0.25 N, Isp (K1) = 1500 s, DecrementMass enabled, Thrust Vector (VNB: X=1, Y=0, Z=0)

Simulation Parameters:
- ForceModel: Earth Point Mass gravity only (Degree 0, Order 0; no drag, SRP, or third-body gravity)
- Mission: Perform a continuous finite burn until Earth.SMA >= 42164.17 km

Outputs:
- Save GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/ep_spiral.script
- Write final values to ~/GMAT_output/ep_spiral_report.txt using these labels:
  elapsed_days: [AeroCom1.ElapsedDays]
  remaining_fuel_kg: [AeroCom1.XenonTank.FuelMass]
  final_sma_km: [AeroCom1.Earth.SMA]
  final_eccentricity: [AeroCom1.Earth.ECC]
  final_inclination_deg: [AeroCom1.Earth.INC]

Login: username=ga, password=password123
